title.headline: The Performance Gap
title.subtitle: Same floor, very different reachable spans.

secondary.label: CPU Compute
secondary.caption: 144x total span
secondary.note: Fit and cost are closer to deployment reality.

axis.label: Bandwidth axis
axis.note: Same ethernet floor under both paths

annotation.label: Bottleneck shift
annotation.note: Memory and topology dominate before peak FLOPs.

floor.note: Ethernet equalizes the floor for every cluster.

thesis.title: Optimize for the constraint, not the headline.
thesis.line_1: The GPU path wins on raw peak throughput but amplifies memory and network pressure.
thesis.line_2: The CPU path stays inside a smaller, more closable deployment gap.

summary.conclusion: The comparison family should make the span itself legible, not just the endpoints.
summary.footer: Broader training should keep values external and let the DSL own only the board structure.
